affected_files.change: Add unexported normalizeTopicPath helper; route ExecuteSplitBtreeInsert, ExecuteSplitAtomic's apply closure, and RecoverSplitCommits's replay loop through it for both oldPath and every newPath before calling tree.Insert.

Add TestSplitBtreeKeyNormalization (equivalent-but-differently-formatted paths -> same canonical key, via ExecuteSplitBtreeInsert + tree.Lookup); extend existing TestExecuteSplitBtreeInsert-adjacent coverage only if needed for regression safety.

packages_not_touched_but_related: engine/btree (Tree.Insert/Lookup consumed unchanged, opaque string key, no API change)
engine/catalog (no call site touches tree.Insert directly)
engine/split/orchestrate.go (no call site touches tree.Insert directly; owned by concurrent 4.5.3.3 fix-cycle, not touched here)
engine/split/guard.go (unrelated, owned by 4.5.3.2, not touched here)
engine/split/split_race_test.go (unrelated, owned by 4.5.3.6, not touched here)

risk_notes: Normalization is a no-op on all existing fixture paths (already canonical), so behavior-preserving for the entire current test suite.

engine/split is documented as 'highest-risk correctness surface in entire engine' (docs/LLD/split.md); mitigated by keeping the change additive/idempotent and running the full engine/split suite under -race.

Concurrent agents are working on orchestrate.go/orchestrate_test.go (fix-cycle) and split_race_test.go in the same package; this change does not touch those files, but a full `go test ./engine/split/...` run may transiently reflect their in-progress state. Scoped test runs will target only tests relevant to this change plus the full package for a final sanity check, per instructions.

subtask 4.5.3.5 (not yet started) will also edit execute_test.go after this commit lands; this run adds one net-new test function and touches no other test in a way that should conflict, but is not this run's concern to coordinate further than committing promptly.
